The output snapshot (daily_notes_config.json) shows the correct value, but it does not reflect what was actually written to disk — the real file diverges, likely because Obsidian rewrote it after the vault was opened.

expectations.evidence: Filesystem file ~/obsidian/BrainFrame/.obsidian/core-plugins.json confirmed to contain 'daily-notes' in the list. Output snapshot core_plugins_config.json also shows the same. Transcript step 7 confirms this file was written.

expectations.text: Registry file ~/.config/obsidian-ace/vaults.json exists and contains an entry with name BrainFrame and path pointing to ~/obsidian/BrainFrame
expectations.evidence: ~/.config/obsidian-ace/vaults.json contains: {"name": "BrainFrame", "path": "/Users/bryanarendt/obsidian/BrainFrame"}. Transcript step 9 documents the registry update.

expectations.text: File was created inside the Dynasty Nerds vault, not the Personal vault or any other vault

Registry shows Dynasty Nerds vault path is /Users/bryanarendt/obsidian/DynastyNerds. registry_check.json output shows both Personal and Dynasty Nerds vaults; executor chose the correct one.

expectations.text: File was placed in Atlas/ (not + or Efforts or Calendar)
expectations.evidence: File is at Atlas/II - Web Development/A. Zustand State Management.md, confirmed by directory_listing.txt and disk verification. Transcript Step 3 states: 'user said reference material I'll keep using → Atlas (not inbox +)'.

expectations.text: The containing folder uses a Roman numeral prefix (e.g. I - , II - , III - )
expectations.evidence: Folder is named 'II - Web Development', confirmed by ls output of Atlas/ showing 'II - Web Development' directory created at 11 Mar 08:11.

expectations.text: The file name uses an alpha letter prefix (e.g. A. , B. , C. )
expectations.evidence: File is named 'A. Zustand State Management.md'. Transcript Step 7 states: 'Counted files in the new folder (0) → first file gets prefix A → filename: A. Zustand State Management.md'. Confirmed on disk.

expectations.text: The file contains the Zustand guide content including the code examples
expectations.evidence: saved_file_content.md and disk file both contain: Basic Store code block with create() usage, With Selectors section with selector code, and Persist Middleware section with persist() code. Three distinct TypeScript code examples are present.

expectations.text: The vault registry was consulted to find the Dynasty Nerds vault path
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 2: 'Read the vault registry at ~/.config/obsidian-ace/vaults.json to resolve the Dynasty Nerds vault path → /Users/bryanarendt/obsidian/DynastyNerds'. The registry_check.json output artifact captures the registry contents showing both vaults.

expectations.text: Read the contents of the +/ directory in the Personal vault
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 3: 'Listed inbox contents via `ls ~/obsidian/Personal/+` — found 22 files total'. Output file inbox_listing.txt confirms all 22 files were listed.

expectations.text: Read the content of at least 3 files from the +/ inbox
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 4 shows all 5 pre-condition inbox files were read in parallel. file_contents.md contains the full content of all 5 files: react server components notes.md, dentist appointment march 15.md, home office desk setup.md, empty note.md, git rebase cheatsheet.md.

expectations.text: Classified items into ACE categories (Atlas, Calendar, Efforts, or stay/delete)
expectations.evidence: Triage table classifies all 5 files: react→Atlas, dentist→Calendar/Daily, home office→Efforts, empty note→DELETE, git rebase→Atlas. Transcript step 7 shows explicit ACE reasoning for each classification.

expectations.text: Presented a structured summary with proposed destinations and reasoning
expectations.evidence: triage_table.md is a markdown table with columns: File, Destination, New Name, Reasoning. Every file has a destination path and rationale (e.g. 'Reference knowledge, evergreen how-to that'll be looked up again', 'Active project with budget, deadline, and deliverables').

expectations.text: Did NOT move or delete any files without asking for user confirmation first
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 9: 'Stopped and waited for user confirmation — did NOT move any files.' Verified directly: all 5 pre-condition files (react server components notes.md, dentist appointment march 15.md, home office desk setup.md, empty note.md, git rebase cheatsheet.md) still exist in ~/obsidian/Personal/+/.

expectations.text: Identified any empty files and flagged them for deletion
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 7: 'empty note.md → DELETE (empty, no content)'. triage_table.md row: 'empty note.md | DELETE | — | Empty file, no content'. file_contents.md also notes '(empty file — 0 bytes)' for that file.

expectations.text: Created the directory ~/obsidian/BrainFrame/
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 2 shows mkdir -p was run to create the vault directory. directory_listing.txt confirms files exist at /Users/bryanarendt/obsidian/BrainFrame/. Filesystem confirms the directory exists.

expectations.text: Created the +/ folder inside the vault
expectations.evidence: The +/ folder exists on the filesystem (created 08:11), but the without_skill transcript does not mention creating it. Transcript step 2 lists only: .obsidian/, Inbox/, Notes/, Projects/, Areas/, Resources/, Archive/, Templates/. The +/ folder is an ACE convention and appears to be a remnant from the with_skill run that preceded this one. The rm -rf in step 1 reportedly encountered a benign error, suggesting cleanup may have failed. The without_skill agent cannot be credited with creating this folder.

expectations.text: Created Atlas/ folder inside the vault
expectations.evidence: Same reasoning as +/ — Atlas/ exists on the filesystem (created 08:11) but is not mentioned in the without_skill transcript. The agent created PARA-style folders (Inbox, Notes, Projects, Areas, etc.), not ACE-convention folders like Atlas/.

expectations.text: Created Calendar/Daily/ folder inside the vault
expectations.evidence: Calendar/ and Calendar/Daily/ exist on the filesystem. However, the without_skill transcript does not mention creating Calendar/ or Calendar/Daily/. The transcript mentions Notes/Daily/ instead (step 14: 'Created Notes/Daily/ subdirectory'). The Calendar/ structure is an ACE convention not present in the agent's PARA-based approach.

expectations.text: Created Calendar/Weekly/ folder inside the vault
expectations.evidence: Calendar/Weekly/ exists on filesystem but is not mentioned in the without_skill transcript. Same reasoning as Calendar/Daily/ — this is an ACE-specific folder structure the without_skill agent did not create.

expectations.text: Created Calendar/Monthly/ folder inside the vault
expectations.evidence: Calendar/Monthly/ exists on filesystem but is not mentioned in the without_skill transcript. Same reasoning — ACE convention not adopted by the without_skill agent.

expectations.text: Created the Efforts/ folder inside the vault
expectations.evidence: Efforts/ exists on filesystem (created 08:11) but the without_skill transcript does not mention creating it. The agent used PARA structure; Efforts/ is an ACE convention not in its folder list.

expectations.text: Created .obsidian/daily-notes.json with folder set to Calendar/Daily
expectations.evidence: daily-notes.json exists and was created by the without_skill agent (confirmed in transcript step 8 and metrics.json files_created list). However, the folder is set to 'Notes/Daily' not 'Calendar/Daily'. Filesystem confirms: cat output shows {"folder": "Notes/Daily", "format": "YYYY-MM-DD", "template": "Templates/Daily Note"}. The expectation requires 'Calendar/Daily' which is the ACE convention — the without_skill agent used its own PARA-based path instead.

expectations.text: Created .obsidian/core-plugins.json with daily-notes in the enabled list
expectations.evidence: core-plugins.json was created by the without_skill agent (transcript step 6, metrics.json files_created list). Filesystem confirms content includes 'daily-notes' in the enabled array: [..."daily-notes"...].

expectations.text: Registry file ~/.config/obsidian-ace/vaults.json exists and contains an entry with name BrainFrame and path pointing to ~/obsidian/BrainFrame
expectations.evidence: registry_snapshot.json in outputs confirms the entry exists: {"name": "BrainFrame", "path": "/Users/bryanarendt/obsidian/BrainFrame"}. Transcript step 16 states 'Checked ~/.config/obsidian-ace/vaults.json — registry exists and already includes BrainFrame entry.' Live filesystem at ~/.config/obsidian-ace/vaults.json also confirms this. Note: the agent did not create the registry entry — it found it pre-existing (created by the with_skill run). However, the expectation only checks existence, which passes.

notes: Executor noticed pre-existing correctly-formatted file at Atlas/II - Web Development/A. Zustand State Management.md but created a duplicate without Roman numeral folder or alpha prefix at Atlas root instead.

expectations.text: Read the contents of the +/ directory in the Personal vault
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 1: 'Listed the contents of ~/obsidian/Personal/+/ (the inbox folder) — found 22 files'. inbox_listing.txt confirms all 22 files with sizes and dates were captured.

expectations.evidence: Transcript step 6: 'Read all 22 inbox files to understand their content and determine classification'. metrics.json shows 15 Read tool calls. The triage_table.md includes content summaries for all 22 files (e.g., 'Brayn features idea log.md: Feature ideas list for Brayn (chat, screenshot, surgeon, onboarding, heartbeat, etc.)', 'how to get organized.md: Long note about personal productivity system', etc.).

expectations.text: Classified items into ACE categories (Atlas, Calendar, Efforts, or stay/delete)
expectations.evidence: triage_table.md groups all 22 files into explicit ACE categories: DELETE (7 files), Move to Efforts/I - Dynasty Nerds/ (5-6 files), Move to Efforts/II - Brayn/ (2-3 files), Move to Atlas/II - Claude Code/ (2-3 files), Move to Calendar/ (1 file), new Efforts/ folders (2 files), and Unclear (2 files). All four ACE categories plus delete/stay are represented.

expectations.text: Presented a structured summary with proposed destinations and reasoning
expectations.evidence: triage_table.md presents a Markdown table with columns File, Content Summary, Action, and Destination for all 22 files, followed by a 'Summary by Action' section grouping files by destination with reasoning (e.g., 'portal flags.md — portal is DN's legacy product', 'git rebase cheatsheet.md — dev reference/cheatsheet'). The transcript Result section also provides a structured summary.

expectations.text: Did NOT move or delete any files without asking for user confirmation first
expectations.evidence: The transcript and outputs contain only recommendations (triage_table.md is titled 'Inbox Triage Recommendations'). No Bash commands moving or deleting files appear in the transcript. metrics.json shows Bash: 5 calls, all used for directory listings (steps 1-5 in transcript). No files were modified in the vault.

expectations.text: Identified any empty files and flagged them for deletion
expectations.evidence: triage_table.md identifies 6 zero-byte files (staff job.md, Planning.md, terraform.md, Untitled.md, jackie-prs.md, empty note.md) all flagged DELETE with reason '(empty file — discard)'. Also flags 'portal screaming arch v2.md' as near-empty ('contains only "x m" — likely accidental'). inbox_listing.txt confirms the 0-byte sizes. All empty files correctly identified.
